onboarding.check_in: All new international students must complete Immigration Check-In with OIS after arriving.
onboarding.sevis: F-1 check-in removes SEVIS/Advising Hold. J-1 must validate within 30 days of DS-2019 start date.
onboarding.orientation: OIS runs International Student Orientation. UIC Global students complete online Blackboard orientation before arrival.
onboarding.placement_tests: UIC Global undergrads must complete placement exams before arriving. Due April 27, 2026.

visa.f1: Most common status. Requires full-time enrollment fall/spring. Allows on-campus work up to 20hr/week.
visa.j1: Exchange visitors. Must get work authorization from sponsor before working.
visa.travel_signature: Must be no more than 1 year old on date of re-entry. Request via myOIS.
visa.reduced_course_load: Exceptions for final semester or 50% assistantship. Must apply to OIS by add/drop deadline.
visa.note: General institutional guidance only — not legal advice

work.on_campus: F-1 can work immediately without OIS authorization. Up to 20hr/week during school, full-time during breaks.
work.cpt: Curricular Practical Training — must be academically required and related to major.
work.opt: Optional Practical Training — post-graduation work in field of study.
work.stem_opt: 24-month extension for STEM degree holders on post-completion OPT.

fees.campuscare: Required. Can waive with equivalent insurance before semester deadline.

study_abroad.office: Study Abroad Office, University Hall Room 502
study_abroad.first_step: Complete First Step presentation before applying.
study_abroad.credit: Study abroad coursework counts as UIC credit, not transfer credit.

faqs.q: Do new international students need to check in with OIS?
faqs.a: Yes, all new international students must complete Immigration Check-In with OIS after arriving.

faqs.q: Can F-1 students work on campus right away?
faqs.a: Yes, without additional OIS authorization, up to 20hr/week during school.

faqs.q: Do study abroad classes count as transfer credit?
faqs.a: No — they count as UIC credit.
Yes, with proof of equivalent insurance before the semester deadline.
